purpose: Machine-readable implementation state for the AI_book-derived Theseus roadmap. This is the governed bridge between roadmap.md prose and registry-owned implementation work.

latest_ai_book_technique_reconciliation.source_basis: AI_book chapter-level Planned Codex test inventories and locally reproduced Theseus evidence

The durable semantic-IR obligation MLX private rung now trains from aggregate private failure obligations and improves heldout LM loss 2.019539 -> 1.467591 with clean public/external/fallback counters, but paired strict decode still scores 0 behavior passes on broad_private_heldout and private_train_replay. The newest graph/operation source-condition adequacy pass tightens admission rather than claiming behavior: prompt-visible graph/type tags now require task-blind graph-walk AST evidence, clip/window operation tags no longer accept shallow summary bodies, and the same train replay moves from emitted-but-wrong rows to zero admitted rows under --require-source-condition-adequacy with graph_walk_evidence and operation_evidence residuals. A dedicated state-event auxiliary head now improves its own private heldout event loss/accuracy, but direct event-head decode bias drops manifest candidate emission from 2 rows to 0 on the same private replay checkpoint. Future DPO/IPO/ORPO/KTO/SimPO, GRPO/RLVR, MTP, diffusion, or scale work must depend on direct prompt/signature body-token generation producing behavior-positive, non-fallback, non-template learned candidates beyond narrow simple-return replay. The follow-up soft compatibility coupling test is also no longer missing: reports/strict_generator_state_event_consistency_smoke_summary_20260706.json implements opt-in body-action/body-operand compatibility losses against private state-event targets, but the bounded canary is RED. LM/event metrics improve while action/operand metrics regress and compatible-role mass worsens, so simple soft coupling must not become a default profile setting. The first architecture-level predicted-state-event body constructor is also now tested: reports/strict_generator_coupled_state_body_constructor_smoke_summary_20260706.json wires an opt-in shared body hidden state conditioned on the model's own event distribution. Scale 0.25 and 0.05 ablations are both RED; LM/transition/event metrics improve, but action/operand losses regress and tensor-update coverage misses the hard gate. A one-step residual event-probability blend is therefore not enough. The executable-span follow-up in reports/strict_generator_executable_span_constructor_smoke_summary_20260706.json adds a dedicated span head and opt-in span-to-hidden constructor; both canaries improve LM/span/event metrics, but the constructor run still regresses body-action metrics, the head-only run regresses action and operand losses, both miss tensor-update coverage, and both checkpoints are rejected.

latest_ai_book_technique_reconciliation.no_claim_boundary: AI_book technique recommendations are planning coverage, not evidence. Routers, tools, templates, semantic renderers, fallback bodies, public benchmark artifacts, and external runtime inference remain noncredit for learned generation.

latest_ai_book_reconciliation.source_state: The current AI_book manifest has 54 chapters and 504 authored Codex tests. The post-v2.1 empirical cycle is complete; its bounded results narrow governed usefulness, routing, rollback, and unlearning claims without promoting any chapter-core claim.
latest_ai_book_reconciliation.decision: Keep the Phase-10 direct-generator floor as the capability critical path while closing four book-derived integration waves: exact source synchronization and durable semantic memory; useful governed execution with effect-complete rollback; ambiguous routing with adaptive deliberation; and full-state update/unlearning/open-ended campaign causality.
latest_ai_book_reconciliation.current_theseus_evidence: Theseus has mature registry, VCM ABI, evidence, routing, update, and assistant record surfaces, but the book lessons are not yet demonstrated together by useful model behavior or complete real-state transitions.
new_required_work: exact manifest order and book-owned source-field synchronization with digest binding
durable semantic objects and graph/vector retrieval inside the VCM ownership boundary
useful governed assistant transactions with independent effect observation and complete rollback inventories
naturally ambiguous routing plus adaptive-deliberation harm and stopping controls
full-state update, best/final checkpoint authority, forgetting, deletion influence, storage erasure, rollback, and invalidation
scalable oversight, assurance graphs, capability commitments, supply-chain/custody, inter-stack exchange, and open-ended campaign contracts
latest_ai_book_reconciliation.no_claim_boundary: Book mapping and record contracts are planning and infrastructure evidence. They do not establish model capability, safe governance, complete rollback, unlearning, scalable oversight, production readiness, AGI, or ASI.

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Reasoning-Trace Faithfulness
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.entry_condition: active now as a claim/evidence boundary
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: distinguish reasoning traces from receipts, compare trace/action consistency, and preserve monitorability limits
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: trace perturbation and action-replay controls quantify when traces predict or fail to predict executed behavior
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: a fluent or internally consistent trace is not an authoritative account of hidden computation

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: World Models and Model-Based Cognition
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.entry_condition: after the core proposer has nonzero verified behavior
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: learned prediction, error/version ledgers, model-predictive control, imagination/search, causal limits, and sim-to-real boundaries
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: a learned world model improves a frozen planning/control task against model-free and deterministic-simulator controls while prediction error remains calibrated
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: prediction accuracy, simulation fidelity, or latent structure alone is not planning capability or real-world safety

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Multi-Agent Systemic Risk and Agent Economies
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.entry_condition: before multi-agent economic or autonomous fleet operation
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: population conflict, collusion, cascades, market behavior, governance graphs, miscoordination, and gradual disempowerment
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: stress simulations and live bounded traces expose correlated failure, collusion, and cascade paths with containment decisions
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: single-agent safety or protocol correctness does not establish population-level stability

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Persuasion, Epistemic Security, and Human Agency
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.entry_condition: only after stronger empirical anchors and an ownership decision
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: approver-directed influence, disclosure/consent, trusted-interface laundering, persuasion thresholds, and epistemic effects
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: controlled studies or source-backed fixtures measure disclosure, consent, influence, and decision-quality effects without covert experimentation
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: one persuasion benchmark cannot establish human parity, broad influence, or compute-scaling claims

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: CAIS, Embedded Agency, and Corrigibility Foundations
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.entry_condition: active as prior-art positioning; chapter only if a distinct mechanism and proof program emerges
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: service composition, embedded-agency limits, formal corrigibility limits, record-level guarantee boundaries, and prior-art deltas
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: source-noted prior-art and explicit weakening conditions narrow Theseus claims and architecture interfaces
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: record-level governance cannot establish corrigibility of learned internal cognition

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Perception, Grounding, and Observation Trust
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.entry_condition: when a vision, audio, or sensor modality begins
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: observation contracts, sensor identity, provenance/taint, fusion disagreement, active-perception budgets, uncertainty, refresh, spoofing, and occlusion
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: multi-source observations carry calibrated uncertainty and disagreement; spoofed, stale, missing, and occluded inputs trigger bounded refresh or abstention
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: sensor availability or representation quality is not grounded understanding

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Embodied Execution and Physical-World Safety
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: control periods, hazard envelopes, interlocks, simulation-before-actuation, safe fallback controllers, stop authority, degraded modes, and irreversible residuals
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: bounded traces prove deadlines/interlocks/fallback/stop paths and retain sim-to-real residuals before any real actuator route
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: simulation success or temporal-logic checks do not prove open-world physical safety

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Operational Resilience, Incident Response, and Decommissioning
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: criticality, dependency maps, impact tolerance, monitoring, containment, failover, manual fallback, recovery, disclosure, post-incident learning, and retirement
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: fault injection exercises containment, degraded operation, recovery objectives, evidence preservation, safety-case invalidation, and secure decommissioning
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: a runbook or passing failover smoke is not operational resilience under untested failure classes

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Privacy, Data Rights, and Multi-Agent Memory
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: purpose/use limitation, minimization, consent, retention, access/correction/export, privacy budgets, inference attacks, internal-channel leakage, cross-user memory, and deletion closure
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: multi-user fixtures enforce purpose, access, export, correction, isolation, privacy budget, and descendant deletion across VCM/training/retrieval paths
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: local storage, encryption, redaction, or deletion requests do not establish privacy or verified unlearning

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Autonomous Replication, Proliferation, and Containment
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.entry_condition: before any replication, compute provisioning, or persistence authority
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: descendant identity, principal binding, resource acquisition, weight/code copying, provisioning, persistence, lineage, credential/network containment, shutdown resistance, and threshold responses
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: replication requests are denied by default and bounded fixtures prove lineage, quotas, revocation, containment, and shutdown across descendants
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: Hive installation or package copying is not autonomous replication capability or evidence of containment

book_future_candidate_crosswalk.title: Scientific Discovery and Experimental Governance
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.entry_condition: when Theseus begins proposing or executing scientific experiments
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.theseus_scope: hypothesis/objective provenance, experiment contracts, instrument/simulator authority, preregistration, independent measurement/analysis, statistical/causal limits, replication, negative results, dual-use review, and evidence handoff
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.acceptance_boundary: a preregistered public-safe experiment replays with independent analysis, negative controls, causal/statistical boundaries, artifact lineage, dual-use decision, and claim-ledger transition
book_future_candidate_crosswalk.non_claim_boundary: a generated hypothesis, simulation, significant result, or one replication is not scientific discovery or broad causal knowledge

book_future_cross_cutting_obligations: calibrated uncertainty, selective prediction, abstention, and reasoning-trace/action faithfulness under Phases 4 and 14
prompt-injection limits, autonomous-insider and collusion probes, randomized monitoring, oversight latency, and operator-load residuals under Phase 18
active perception, OOD state, fusion disagreement, causal/counterfactual reasoning, and structural-identifiability boundaries under Phases 13 and 17
temporal-logic monitoring, interlocks, fallback controllers, degraded operation, and sim-to-real residuals under Phases 17 and 18
AIBOM/reproducible builds/derivative revocation plus privacy budgets, cross-user leakage, and deletion propagation under Phases 0, 3, 7, 14, and 18
objective fingerprints, periodic recontracting, time-horizon and automated-R&D measures, dual-use profiles, provider concentration, portability, and shutdown handoff under Phases 2, 8, 9, and 18

weekly_focus_decision.recommendation: Spend the remaining weekly token budget primarily on Project Theseus while keeping AI_book as the specification, evidence ledger, and publication surface.

implementation_rules: Improve an existing registered surface before creating a new surface.
Bind every phase to an abstraction, implementation, and VIEA execution-spine hook before claiming it is wired.
Do not call a phase implemented from prose, private 1.0 scores, or self-declared flags alone.
Every completed phase must leave runnable gates, evidence reports, docs, and typed failure behavior.
Book-derived phases 13-19 are preserved; they may be sequenced, narrowed, or scaffolded, but not deleted from the roadmap.
At most one flagship implementation lane may be active at a time; other lanes can do maintenance, evidence sync, or narrow dependency work only.
Every capability statement must carry a support state: argument, prototype-backed, synthetic-test-backed, empirical-test-backed, or replayable-reference-backed.
Every authored AI_book chapter must map to a Theseus implementation slice with a minimum slice, beyond-SOTA endpoint, support-state target, no-claim boundary, and gate or fixture.

AI_book chapter-level Planned Codex tests are treated as missing technique obligations until a registered Theseus implementation has matched controls, negative controls, no-cheat audit, retained residuals, and explicit non-claims.
Negative, inconclusive, and regression evidence must remain attached to the claim or phase it constrains; do not replace a negative report with a nearby positive family.

Before training becomes the primary roadmap focus again, the book-reference core must be represented as runnable claim-ledger, replacement-transaction, authority/SCIF, assistant-product, and evidence-hygiene slices.

AI_book technique recommendations are coverage obligations, but if a technique has already produced clean negative behavior evidence, the roadmap must route to the lower-level wall before retrying it.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book Chapter 38 planned tests plus locally reproduced Theseus evidence
planned_codex_test_backlog.dependency: strict prompt/signature body-token replay must emit non-fallback, nontrivial, loadable candidates before another preference sweep can mean anything behaviorally
planned_codex_test_backlog.acceptance_gate: A bounded DPO/IPO/ORPO/KTO/SimPO comparison uses the same private accepted/rejected split, frozen reference checkpoint, heldout private verifier, reward-hacking probes, drift bounds, and rollback plan.
planned_codex_test_backlog.no_claim_boundary: Loss or policy-gap movement is not capability. No public benchmark rows, answer templates, fallback bodies, routers, deterministic tools, or semantic renderers count as learned generation.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book Chapter 38 planned tests and LongRLVR source notes
planned_codex_test_backlog.dependency: requires usable learned candidates and a verifier-capacity ledger before online/group-relative reward optimization
planned_codex_test_backlog.acceptance_gate: Run reward-present versus reward-removed ablations on private exact-verifier tasks with rollout budget, reward-hacking probes, drift bound, rollback receipt, verifier-cost ledger, and residual mining.
planned_codex_test_backlog.no_claim_boundary: A verifier-reward run cannot expand authority, cannot train on public benchmark artifacts, and cannot become a default route until heldout private behavior improves under replay.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book Chapter 38 multi-target optimization
planned_codex_test_backlog.dependency: requires behavior-change leases with admissible feedback and per-target heldouts
planned_codex_test_backlog.acceptance_gate: Planner, router, VCM selector, verifier policy, executor policy, and generation-mode selector updates each carry target policy, feedback source, authority effect, rollback, monitor window, and task-specific heldout checks.
planned_codex_test_backlog.no_claim_boundary: A policy update is not allowed to hide displaced verification, repair, human-cleanup cost, or authority expansion.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book Chapters 15 and 30 planned tests
planned_codex_test_backlog.dependency: requires candidate integrity and semantic atom mapping for actual generated failures
planned_codex_test_backlog.acceptance_gate: Candidate states transition through generated, verified_exact, verified_lossy, repaired_exact, literal_fallback_noncredit, and quarantined with S/R/Q/G/V/E consistency checks, verifier receipts, repair cost, fallback count, and residual obligations.
planned_codex_test_backlog.no_claim_boundary: Fallback or repaired output may be useful product behavior but never learned-generation promotion evidence unless independent integrity proves the learned candidate itself produced it.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book fast-generation chapters and multi-token prediction source notes
planned_codex_test_backlog.dependency: requires a nonzero behavior baseline so useful verified output per second can be measured
planned_codex_test_backlog.acceptance_gate: MTP is compared against a matched single-token AR control on the same private data, active compute, verifier, and decode budget, reporting accepted spans, verifier pass, useful-solution/sec, residuals, and regressions.
planned_codex_test_backlog.no_claim_boundary: Proposed-token throughput, lower LM loss, or larger candidate count alone is not speed or capability evidence.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book fast-generation chapters plus Medusa/EAGLE/speculative/LayerSkip notes
planned_codex_test_backlog.dependency: requires generation-mode registry accounting and accepted-output scoring
planned_codex_test_backlog.acceptance_gate: Each mode has a generation_mode_record with active compute, cache policy, verification cost, accepted-output quality, useful-solution/sec, fallback count, and matched AR baseline.
planned_codex_test_backlog.no_claim_boundary: Fast-generation helpers do not count as learned correctness unless verifier-accepted behavior improves under matched controls.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book diffusion/LLaDA planned tests
planned_codex_test_backlog.dependency: typed semantic IR and localized dependent-atom replay are implemented and reduce type-handling faults, but intended-behavior lift remains zero; sketch/infill work waits for direct learned full-body candidates with behavior-positive private verifier evidence

planned_codex_test_backlog.no_claim_boundary: Sketch-first repair cannot claim learned generation if a deterministic renderer, hidden target field, public artifact, or fallback body supplies the answer.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book Chapters 16-17 planned tests
planned_codex_test_backlog.dependency: requires deployed resolver/compiler conformance beyond fixture-level VCM records
planned_codex_test_backlog.acceptance_gate: Every materialized context packet carries source refs, omissions, loss contract, permitted uses, authority ceiling, taint/deletion closure, copy-on-write branch lineage, typed mandatory-miss faults, and consumer policy.
planned_codex_test_backlog.no_claim_boundary: A summary, cache hit, or retrieved packet cannot raise the authority ceiling of its source or claim model-native KV/prefix runtime parity without separate native lifecycle proof.

planned_codex_test_backlog.source_basis: AI_book Chapter 18 planned tests

current_non_claims: Phase 10 has a clean experimental transformer successor, but no qualified private proposer floor, broad model-quality, runtime-serving, or public-transfer win.

The prior 1/24 floor is invalid because private tests influenced inferred callable arity. A later ID-space audit found split source IDs shifted into the target embedding segment. The repaired mapping raises independently integrity-verified candidates from 38/88 to 57/87 and reduces inert stubs from 50 to 30, but covers only 22 tasks and remains 0/24. The completed training receipt was overwritten by the old evaluation-only CLI path, so the canonical gate also records incomplete training provenance. Wider beam, shared vocabulary, two-million-position continuation, and corrected source offsets all remain non-promoted.

The old Rust/template-like fanout inventory, action routers, semantic renderers, private ngrams, deterministic tools, and fallback returns remain diagnostic/tool baselines only and do not support learned-generation promotion claims.

The legacy DPO replay and the current canonical DPO canary are both negative behavior evidence. The current exact-pair run moved preference margin +1.803796 but regressed heldout behavior 1/24 -> 0/24 while the reward-removed control preserved 1/24; both reward checkpoints remain quarantined and cannot support default routing, public transfer, or learned-generation promotion.

The direct body-emission and state-transition profiles are training evidence only: direct body emission matched 128/128 private rows and 8054 token positions, local-return closure matched 79/128 rows and 1967 positions, and closed-state transition matched 127/128 rows and 9637 positions, but the paired broad strict replays still have 0 main candidate rows or 0 behavior passes.

The guarded/default static relaxation is narrow behavior evidence on existing private simple-return replay only: it is not a public-transfer claim, not a promotion claim, and not evidence that loop/action or broad expression semantics are solved.

The strict decode hygiene and generated-state return/dependency guards block malformed isinstance first-argument chains, bare builtin type runtime values, constant-only conditions, top-level returns that ignore visible inputs, and missing finalizer tokens for model-created visible-input accumulators; this is pathology-removal evidence only, because the paired broad canary still emits 0 learned rows and 0/4 behavior passes.

The source-plan compatibility reranker is diagnostic adequacy evidence only: it uses prompt-visible source tags and moves first-plan choices on the strict two-row canary, but still emits zero manifest candidate rows and zero behavior passes. It is not learned-generation credit, not a renderer, and not a promotion route.

The body-action head is trainable structural-role evidence only: it activates over private body-continuation positions and can bias decode, but the first canary worsens heldout action loss/accuracy and strict replay still emits zero manifest rows with zero behavior passes. The two generated runtime-loaded verifier attempts are diagnostic shape movement, not admitted learned-generation improvement.

The body-operand/value binding head is trainable role evidence only: it improves heldout operand accuracy but not operand loss. When the existing learned-prefix adequacy route is enabled, the same checkpoint emits integrity-verified nontrivial transformer/hybrid rows, but behavior remains 0-pass and production routing fails closed on quality.

The operation-adequacy decode pass is diagnostic/ranking evidence only: it now reports missing_expected_update_call when the learned prefix predicts a call update but the body emits only assignment transforms, ranks prompt-visible operation evidence ahead of generic loop-score proxies, and lets prompt-visible operation tags propose operation-bearing branch/update tokens before the generic loop path preempts them. The bounded private canaries remain clean but behavior-zero: 4 integrity-verified generated rows, 0 integrity mismatches, nontrivial-return rate 1.0, and 0/2 private passes. The follow-up zero-credit semantic-operation value-construction canary preserves clean candidate integrity and emits 2 nontrivial transformer/hybrid private-eval rows, but still scores 0/2 private passes and exposes repeated max(max(... starvation plus shallow accumulator/type-handling failures rather than operation-bearing clamp/round or absolute-distance bodies.

The next governed step is broad semantic expression/body construction repair through semantic IR localized repair or a trainable AST/state-transition head, not another target-side weighting family, source-condition ranker, or diagnostic guard. Prompt/signature body-token decode now produces admitted non-fallback, non-template, nontrivial candidates under the canonical prefix-governed route, but those candidates are semantically shallow and must pass private verifier behavior before preference/RL scaling. DPO/IPO/ORPO/KTO/SimPO and GRPO/RLOO/ReMax/RLVR remain downstream of verifier behavior, not substitutes for it.

The state-machine event target canary is residual/training-signal evidence only: it activates a sharper private event policy and improves LM plus transition loss, but action/operand metrics still fail and no checkpoint is routeable. It does not support learned-generation promotion, public-transfer claims, or default routing.

The executable-span canary is also residual/training-signal evidence only: the dedicated span head and opt-in span-to-hidden body constructor improve LM/span/event metrics, but action/operand or tensor-update gates still fail and no checkpoint is routeable. It does not support learned-generation promotion, public-transfer claims, or default routing.

book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.book_minimal_implementation: A costed route ledger with quality predicates, residual accounting, and fallback criteria.
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.book_beyond_state_of_art: The mature endpoint is a governed route economy for cognition, not a larger model, a cheaper model, or a single acceleration trick. Conditional-computation systems, model cascades, compression schemes, fast-generation methods, and broad benchmark suites each attack one cost surface; the Efficient ASI endpoint makes those surfaces comparable through a shared route ledger for task predicates, authority ceilings, selected and rejected candidates, visible cost classes, verification, fallback, residuals, and support-state effects.
book_interfaces: Planning requests capability profiles.
Routing selects specialists.
Evidence decides whether efficiency claims survived quality and cost tests.
book_invariants: Efficiency claims include quality and cost.

book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.part_title: Part IV - Evidence, Implementation, and the Living Book
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.book_minimal_implementation: A viable first slice is a versioned data-admission receipt, a public-safe synthetic fixture, deterministic routing for missing controls, and a lineage ledger that keeps every candidate experimental until an independently evaluated training or deletion path is accepted.
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.book_beyond_state_of_art: The mature target is a stack-wide data governance plane that treats every learning input, synthetic descendant, replay item, deletion request, evaluation split, and derived model artifact as an auditable lifecycle object. It measures workload-specific coverage, contamination, utility, cost, privacy, freshness, and deletion propagation; compares replacement, accumulation, replay, quarantine, and retraining policies; and requires a verifier-backed lineage closure before a result can affect deployment or belief. This is a target architecture, not a current system result: support remains argument until scoped intake, training, unlearning, and descendant-verification evidence is independently replayed and accepted.

book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.part_title: Part IV - Evidence, Implementation, and the Living Book
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.book_minimal_implementation: A `PROJECT_STEWARD.yml` file plus a GitHub-oriented steward bot that labels issues, drafts plans, proposes work contracts, requests CI, summarizes pull requests, writes release notes, records evidence, opens sunset reviews, and never spends funds or merges to protected branches without policy approval.
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.book_beyond_state_of_art: A durable-artifact stack ultimately needs a project-lifecycle operating system. The steward would not be a project owner, treasurer, maintainer monopoly, or autonomous manager with implied rights. It would be a bounded continuity service that keeps mission, memory, roadmap, work, funds, compute, contributors, evidence, releases, governance, and sunset criteria inspectable across many human and AI work cycles. A mature steward OS would let every durable artifact carry an Artifact Steward Charter plus typed records for work contracts, contribution ledgers, treasury policy, event taint, steward action decisions, release evidence, federation contracts, and sunset review. The steward would propose, prepare, coordinate, and execute only inside explicit authority. Humans, maintainers, communities, project hives, personal hives, CI runners, and rented compute would receive scoped contracts rather than inheriting project authority.

PlanForge and Talos turn goals into milestones, issues, jobs, reviews, artifacts, and releases.
Spinoza, Benchmaxxing, and evidence ledgers decide whether submitted work changes claim or readiness state.
TokenMana and treasury policies bound spending, compute rental, bounties, and recurring operations.
SCF, governance rights, and constitutional predicates prevent steward authority from replacing owner, maintainer, contributor, or community authority.
book_invariants: The steward is not the owner.
Mission, non-goals, and sunset criteria remain durable.
Spending is a bounded actuator governed by treasury policy.

book_failure_modes: Mission drift from optimizing activity rather than purpose.

Governance capture by large holders, early maintainers, or steward-controlled scoring.
Agentic workflow injection through untrusted repository event context.
AI maintainer monopoly that makes the project opaque to humans.
Zombie project continuation after user value disappears.

A mature living-book workflow would let a future agent ingest a new AI paper, mine its public-safe content, decide whether to update a chapter or add a precise new one, update the outline and manifest, generate proof/test backlog, draft or revise prose, validate source/evidence boundaries, render the live site, and prepare reader editions without hand-renumbering or hidden support-state changes.
no_claims: roadmap mapping is not implementation completion
book design argument is not model-capability evidence
tool/template/router output is not learned generation
public benchmark artifacts remain calibration-only and never training rows

book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.chapter_title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.part_title: Part IV - Evidence, Implementation, and the Living Book
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.book_minimal_implementation: A split Corben-source-corpus and external-literature plan with source-note backlog and insertion/merge rules.
book_chapter_implementation_crosswalk.book_beyond_state_of_art: The bibliography endpoint is a source and research graph for the entire ASI Stack. The bibliography would not be an afterthought at the end of the book, and it would not blur Corben's papers, Corben-supplied materials, recovered project records, or local project sources with outside literature. It would be the routing table that tells future agents which ideas have sources, which sources have been read, which chapters they affect, which proof or experiment backlogs they create, which material is external, and which claims still cannot move. A mature source graph would give every paper, project, local note, external reference, conversation-mined packet, recovery item, and missing source a public-safety policy, ingestion state, source note, chapter assignments, deduplication status, evidence-transition preconditions, proof/test backlog, citation status, and non-claims. New material would update the research graph before it changes manuscript prose.
book_interfaces: Appendix G is Corben's own papers, Corben-supplied materials, recovered project records, and local project records, while Appendix H is a separate appendix for external sources by other authors and organizations.
Source notes support chapter drafting.
Book outline tells future agents what to mine.
book_invariants: Do not cite unread sources as support.
Do not infer bibliographic metadata.
New papers update inventory before chapter claims.
book_failure_modes: Duplicate chapter creation for overlapping ideas.
